wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the finTracker project overview section. Explain the purpose of this personal finance management application, its target audience, and key value propositions. Document the core functionality including real-time transaction logging, financial visualization, and cross-device synchronization. Describe the technology stack (Next.js 16, React 19, Radix UI, Recharts) and architectural approach. Include the project's mission to help users manage monthly income and expenses with intelligent categorization. Provide context about the mobile-first design philosophy and local-first data persistence strategy. Address the application's internationalization support for multiple currencies and its focus on user experience optimization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Financial Calculations and Analytics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for finTracker's financial calculation engine. Detail the currency conversion system including UAH, USD, and EUR with their respective exchange rates and symbols. Document the formatting utilities for displaying monetary values with proper localization and currency symbols. Explain the category-based financial analytics including income vs expense tracking, spending breakdown by category, and percentage calculations. Cover the date handling system for month navigation, period formatting, and historical data management. Document the mathematical operations for financial summaries, totals, and ratios. Include examples of currency conversion calculations, financial ratio computations, and data aggregation patterns. Address precision handling for monetary calculations and performance considerations for real-time analytics.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for finTracker's component architecture. Detail the hierarchical structure starting with the main FinanceTracker component as the root container, explaining how it orchestrates child components including FinanceHeader, BalanceCard, SummaryCards, TransactionForm, SpendingChart, and TransactionList. Document the component composition patterns, prop drilling strategies, and state sharing mechanisms between parent and child components. Explain the mobile-first component design approach and how components adapt to different screen sizes. Include component lifecycle management, rendering optimization strategies, and the integration of animation libraries like Framer Motion. Document the component boundaries, data flow from parent to children, and how each component contributes to the overall user experience. Address component reusability patterns and the modular design that enables easy maintenance and extension.

wiki_catalogs.name: FinanceTracker Main Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the FinanceTracker main component, which serves as the central orchestrator of the entire personal finance application. Detail the component's role as the primary state manager, coordinating all child components and handling global application logic. Document the extensive state management including transactions, balances, plans, currencies, and UI states. Explain the sophisticated data persistence strategy using localStorage with automatic synchronization across browser tabs. Cover the component's lifecycle management, including hydration from localStorage, automatic recurring transaction generation, and cross-component communication patterns. Document the floating action button (FAB) integration, bottom sheet modal system, and settings management. Include detailed examples of state updates, event handlers, and the relationship between local state and persistent storage. Address performance optimizations, memory management, and error handling strategies. Provide practical examples of component usage, customization options, and integration patterns with other parts of the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Local Storage Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for finTracker's local storage integration system. Detail the data persistence strategy using localStorage for offline functionality and cross-session data retention. Document the key-value storage approach with specific keys for transactions, balances, plans, recurring templates, quick templates, and currency preferences. Explain the data hydration process from localStorage to React state during component initialization and the automatic synchronization mechanisms that keep localStorage and component state in sync. Cover the transaction storage format, including the month-based key structure (finance_YYYY_MM) and transaction object schema with properties like id, amount, category, type, date, and recurringId. Document the balance storage system for card, cash, and savings accounts. Include error handling strategies for localStorage quota exceeded, corrupted data, and browser compatibility issues. Provide examples of data retrieval, storage operations, and recovery procedures for common failure scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Currency Conversion System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for finTracker's currency conversion system. Document the three supported currencies (UAH, USD, EUR) with their fixed exchange rates and symbols. Explain the convertFromUAH function that converts amounts to target currencies using predefined rates. Detail the currency code enumeration and symbol mapping system. Include examples of currency conversion calculations showing how UAH amounts are converted to USD and EUR equivalents. Document the mathematical precision handling and rounding behavior. Address the business logic behind fixed exchange rates and their implications for financial calculations. Provide usage examples demonstrating currency conversion in transaction processing and financial summaries.

wiki_catalogs.name: Base Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for finTracker's base UI components. Document the foundational components including Button variants with all styling options, Input components with validation states, Form components with React Hook Form integration, Select dropdowns, Dialog modals, Card containers, and Table components. Detail each component's props interface, variant options, size configurations, and styling patterns. Include practical examples showing component usage, customization options, and integration patterns. Document accessibility features, keyboard navigation support, and screen reader compatibility. Cover component composition patterns, slot usage with Radix UI, and Tailwind CSS class customization. Address responsive design considerations, animation support, and cross-browser compatibility. Provide guidelines for extending components and maintaining design system consistency.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backup and Export Functionality
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for finTracker's backup and export system. Detail the FinanceBackup data structure including version control, exportedAt timestamp, and data organization. Explain the exportAllData() function implementation covering localStorage enumeration, data filtering by key prefixes, JSON serialization, and file generation. Document the backup file format specification including version 1 schema, data structure for transactions and plans, and export metadata. Include examples of generated backup files, error handling for malformed entries, and browser compatibility considerations. Address security implications of local file downloads and data exposure risks.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for finTracker. Include detailed prerequisites covering Node.js version requirements, npm/yarn installation, and development environment setup. Provide step-by-step installation instructions from repository cloning to dependency installation. Document the development server startup process with 'npm run dev' command and initial setup verification. Explain the project structure overview for new developers, including the Next.js App Router architecture and file organization. Include basic usage examples showing how to add transactions, view financial summaries, and navigate the interface. Address common setup issues and provide troubleshooting guidance for first-time users. Document the build process for production deployment and basic configuration options.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Transfer and Synchronization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for finTracker's data transfer system covering backup, export, and import functionality. Document the backup format specification including version control, data structure, and validation schemas. Explain the cross-device synchronization mechanism using localStorage as the primary data store. Detail the import validation process including data integrity checks, format verification, and error handling strategies. Cover the export functionality for data portability and backup restoration. Document the data migration patterns for backward compatibility and version updates. Include examples of backup file structure, import validation scenarios, and synchronization conflict resolution. Address security considerations for data export and import operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for finTracker's state management architecture. Explain the centralized state approach using React hooks within the FinanceTracker component, covering local state management for transactions, balances, user preferences, and UI state. Document the localStorage synchronization strategy that automatically persists state changes and handles hydration on application load. Detail the state flow patterns from user interactions through form submissions to automatic data persistence. Explain the observer pattern implementation for real-time data synchronization across browser tabs and device refreshes. Cover state normalization strategies, memoization techniques using useMemo and useCallback, and performance optimizations. Document the state migration patterns for handling data schema changes over time. Include error handling strategies for localStorage failures, data corruption recovery, and state validation mechanisms. Address the balance between centralized state management and component-level state for optimal performance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Transaction Form Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the TransactionForm component responsible for smart transaction entry and validation. Document the component's dual-mode operation for both adding new transactions and editing existing ones. Explain the intelligent amount parsing system that handles various numeric formats and currency normalization. Detail the category selection dropdown with emoji representations and color coding. Cover the quick template system integration allowing users to rapidly apply predefined transaction patterns. Document the recurring transaction functionality with template matching and automatic generation. Explain the clipboard integration for smart transaction parsing and extraction. Include comprehensive examples of form validation, error handling, and user interaction patterns. Document the component's props interface, event handlers, and integration with the parent FinanceTracker component. Address accessibility features, keyboard navigation, and mobile-optimized input handling. Provide guidance on customization options, validation rules, and extending functionality with additional template types.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backup and Export System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for finTracker's backup and export system. Document the data transfer utilities that enable cross-device synchronization and data portability. Explain the backup file format specification including version control, data structure validation, and integrity checking mechanisms. Detail the export functionality that generates downloadable JSON backup files containing all financial data, settings, and configurations. Cover the import process with comprehensive validation, error handling, and conflict resolution strategies when importing data from external sources. Document the SettingsModal integration that provides import/export functionality through the user interface. Include examples of backup file structure, validation rules, and troubleshooting common import/export issues such as malformed JSON, version mismatches, and data corruption. Address security considerations for backup files and provide guidance on safe data sharing practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Monetary Formatting and Display
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for finTracker's monetary formatting system. Detail the formatUAH function that handles localized number formatting with Ukrainian locale settings. Document the automatic fraction digit handling based on whether values are whole numbers or decimals. Explain the sign display options (+/- prefixes) and currency symbol integration. Cover the locale-specific formatting rules for Ukrainian notation. Include examples of formatted output for various monetary values including edge cases like zero, negative amounts, and fractional currencies. Document the integration with currency conversion and display consistency across different views. Address internationalization considerations and potential customization options for other locales.

wiki_catalogs.name: Specialized Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for finTracker's specialized business components. Document CategorySelect dropdown for financial categorization, BalanceCard for displaying financial summaries, FinanceHeader for navigation and controls, TransactionForm for smart transaction entry with clipboard integration, TransactionList for displaying and managing transactions, SpendingChart for financial visualization, SummaryCards for key metrics display, and ThemeProvider for dark/light mode switching. Detail each component's specific business logic, data handling patterns, and integration with the finance domain. Include usage examples demonstrating proper implementation, state management integration, and data flow patterns. Document component-specific props, event handlers, and customization options. Address performance considerations, accessibility features, and mobile optimization. Provide guidelines for extending specialized components and integrating them with the broader application architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: Import and Validation System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for finTracker's import and validation system. Document the importDataFromFile() function including File API integration, FileReader asynchronous processing, and JSON parsing workflow. Explain the comprehensive validation schema checking version compatibility, data structure integrity, and type safety. Detail the error handling mechanisms for invalid formats, malformed data, and parsing failures. Cover the data migration process including existing data cleanup, selective key removal, and safe data insertion. Include examples of valid and invalid backup files, common import errors and their resolutions, and validation failure scenarios with specific error messages.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for finTracker's system design. Describe the overall architecture built on Next.js App Router with React 19 components. Document the component hierarchy starting from the main FinanceTracker component and its relationship with child components like TransactionForm, TransactionList, and SpendingChart. Explain the state management strategy using centralized state with localStorage persistence. Detail the data flow patterns from user interactions through form submissions to automatic data synchronization. Document the provider pattern implementation for theme management and the observer pattern for localStorage synchronization. Include system boundaries, component interactions, and integration patterns with external APIs. Address the mobile-first design architecture and responsive component composition. Explain technical decisions around local-first data persistence, performance optimization strategies, and cross-platform compatibility considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Models and Structures
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for finTracker's core data models and structures. Detail the Transaction interface including id, amount, category, type, date, and optional fields like name and recurringId. Document the CategoryInfo structure with name, emoji, icon name, and color properties. Explain the category management system including predefined categories for income and expenses with their emoji representations and color coding. Cover the type definitions for TransactionType, CurrencyCode, and CategoryIconName. Document the data validation rules, constraints, and business logic for each model. Include examples of data structure usage, model instantiation, and data transformation patterns. Address data integrity measures and validation strategies for maintaining consistent financial records.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for finTracker's data flow architecture. Document the unidirectional data flow from user interactions through component boundaries to state updates and persistence. Explain the transaction lifecycle from form submission through validation, state mutation, and localStorage persistence. Detail the event-driven architecture where user actions trigger state changes that propagate through the component hierarchy. Document the data transformation pipeline from raw user input to structured transaction objects, including amount parsing, category assignment, and date formatting. Explain the computed data patterns for financial calculations, chart data preparation, and summary generation. Cover the async data flow for backup operations, import/export functionality, and cross-device synchronization. Include error propagation strategies, validation feedback loops, and the rollback mechanisms for failed operations. Address the data flow optimization techniques and how the application maintains consistency across multiple state updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Transaction List Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the TransactionList component that handles the display, editing, and deletion of financial transactions. Document the component's role in presenting transaction data in an organized, sortable, and filterable format. Explain the edit mode functionality that allows users to modify existing transactions with proper validation and state management. Detail the delete confirmation process and data integrity maintenance. Cover the filtering and sorting capabilities based on date, category, amount, and type. Document the responsive design implementation for mobile devices and touch interaction patterns. Include examples of transaction rendering, event handling for edit/delete actions, and integration with the parent component's state management. Address performance considerations for large transaction datasets, virtual scrolling if implemented, and efficient re-rendering strategies. Document accessibility features, keyboard navigation support, and screen reader compatibility. Provide guidance on customizing display formats, adding new columns, and implementing additional filtering options.

wiki_catalogs.name: Financial Data Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for finTracker's financial entities and structures. Detail the Transaction interface with all properties including id, amount, category, type (income/expense), date, name, and recurringId. Document the RecurringTemplate structure for automated transaction generation with properties for template identification, amount, category, type, and optional name. Explain the QuickTemplate model used for transaction shortcuts with label, amount, and category fields. Cover the CurrencyCode enumeration supporting UAH, USD, and EUR with automatic conversion and formatting capabilities. Document the category system including predefined categories with emoji representations, color coding, and hierarchical organization. Detail the data validation rules, type constraints, and business logic for each model. Include examples of data structures, transformation functions, and utility methods for working with financial data. Address data lifecycle management, immutability patterns, and state mutation strategies used throughout the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Financial Analytics and Aggregation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for finTracker's financial analytics engine. Detail the category-based financial analysis including income vs expense tracking and spending breakdown by category. Document the mathematical operations for calculating totals, percentages, and financial ratios. Explain the aggregation patterns for monthly financial summaries and trend analysis. Cover the data structures used for financial calculations including category hierarchies and transaction grouping. Include examples of analytics computations such as total income calculation, expense distribution by category, and net worth tracking. Document the performance considerations for real-time analytics and large datasets. Address the integration with the UI components for displaying financial insights and charts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Composition and Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for finTracker's component composition patterns and architectural principles. Detail the utility functions for class name merging, component extension patterns, and custom hook integration. wiki_catalogs.name: Cross-Device Synchronization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for finTracker's cross-device synchronization mechanism. Explain how localStorage serves as the primary data store for offline-first architecture and device synchronization. Document the automatic data persistence patterns, localStorage change event handling, and real-time synchronization across browser tabs. Detail the data key organization including finance_monthKey format for transactions and plan_key format for recurring plans. Cover conflict resolution strategies, data consistency guarantees, and synchronization timing. wiki_catalogs.name: Core Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for finTracker's core components. Detail the main FinanceTracker component as the central orchestrator managing state and coordinating child components. Document the Observer pattern implementation for localStorage synchronization, covering event listeners and state propagation mechanisms. Detail the Factory pattern usage in component creation and data transformation utilities. Explain the Strategy pattern applied in currency conversion, date formatting, and category management. Cover the Template Method pattern in transaction processing workflows and the Command pattern in form submission handlers. Document the Singleton pattern implementation for utility functions and the Adapter pattern for integrating third-party libraries like Recharts. wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# FinTracker - Personal Finance Management Application

## Project Introduction

FinTracker is a modern, mobile-first personal finance tracking application built with Next.js 16 and React 19. The application provides users with a comprehensive solution for managing their monthly income and expenses, featuring real-time financial insights, automated categorization, and seamless data synchronization across devices.

### Purpose Statement
The primary goal of FinTracker is to simplify personal financial management by providing an intuitive interface for tracking daily expenses and income while offering meaningful insights into spending patterns and budget forecasting.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Real-time Financial Tracking**: Enable users to quickly log transactions with intelligent categorization
- **Visual Financial Insights**: Provide interactive charts and summaries for spending analysis
- **Cross-device Synchronization**: Maintain consistent data across multiple devices using localStorage
- **Mobile-First Experience**: Deliver an optimal experience for mobile users with gesture-based interactions
- **Data Portability**: Support import/export functionality for backup and migration

### Target Audience
- Individuals seeking personal finance management solutions
- Mobile-first users who prefer touch-optimized interfaces
- Privacy-conscious users who prefer local data storage over cloud services
- Ukrainians managing finances in Ukrainian Hryvnia (₴) with multi-currency support

## Technical Architecture

The application follows a component-based architecture with clear separation of concerns between presentation, business logic, and data persistence layers.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Next.js App Router] --> B[FinanceTracker Component]
    B --> C[UI Components]
    B --> D[Finance Logic]
    B --> E[Data Persistence]
    
    C --> F[Transaction Form]
    C --> G[Spending Chart]
    C --> H[Transaction List]
    C --> I[Summary Cards]
    
    D --> J[Finance Utils]
    D --> K[Category Management]
    D --> L[Currency Conversion]
    
    E --> M[Local Storage]
    E --> N[Backup System]
    
    O[Radix UI Primitives] --> C
    P[Framer Motion] --> C
    Q[Recharts] --> G
    R[Lucide Icons] --> C
```

### Component Breakdown

The application is structured around several key architectural patterns:

**State Management Pattern**: Centralized state management using React hooks with localStorage persistence for offline-first functionality.

**Component Composition**: Modular UI components built with Radix UI primitives for accessibility and consistency.

**Data Flow Architecture**: Unidirectional data flow with automatic persistence and validation layers.

### Design Patterns

1. **Provider Pattern**: Theme provider and state providers manage global application state
2. **Factory Pattern**: Dynamic component creation for transaction templates and categories
3. **Observer Pattern**: Automatic localStorage synchronization for real-time updates
4. **Strategy Pattern**: Pluggable currency conversion and formatting strategies

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

The application uses Next.js 16's App Router with a clean entry point structure:

- **Application Shell**: `app/layout.tsx` - Root layout with metadata and analytics integration
- **Main Page**: `app/page.tsx` - Single-page application mounting point
- **Global Styles**: `app/globals.css` and `styles/globals.css` - Tailwind-based styling foundation

### Core Modules

**Finance Management System** (`lib/finance.ts`):
- Comprehensive category system with emoji representations
- Multi-currency support (UAH, USD, EUR) with automatic conversion
- Transaction modeling with recurring transaction capabilities
- Date-based organization for monthly financial records

**Data Transfer System** (`lib/data-transfer.ts`):
- Backup format specification for version 1 compatibility
- Import/export functionality with validation
- Cross-device synchronization capabilities

**Component Architecture** (`components/`):
- Modular UI components built with Radix UI primitives
- Custom hooks for mobile optimization and toast notifications
- Reusable form components with validation and accessibility features

### Configuration Approach

**Build Configuration** (`next.config.mjs`):
- Next.js 16 configuration with experimental features
- PostCSS integration for advanced CSS processing
- Performance optimizations for production builds

**Development Dependencies** (`package.json`):
- TypeScript 5.7.3 for type safety
- Tailwind CSS 4.2.0 for utility-first styling
- React Hook Form 7.54.1 for form management
- Recharts 2.15.0 for data visualization

### External Dependencies

The application leverages a carefully curated set of dependencies:

**UI Framework**: Radix UI primitives for accessible component foundations
**Animation**: Framer Motion 12.38.0 for smooth transitions and gestures
**Charts**: Recharts 2.15.0 for responsive data visualization
**Icons**: Lucide React 0.564.0 for consistent iconography
**Form Handling**: React Hook Form 7.54.1 with Zod validation
**Analytics**: Vercel Analytics 1.6.1 for performance monitoring

### Integration Points

**Local Storage Integration**: Primary data persistence layer with automatic synchronization
**Clipboard Integration**: Smart paste functionality for quick transaction entry
**File System Integration**: Import/export of financial data as JSON backups
**System Fonts**: Google Fonts integration via Next.js font optimization

### Component Relationship Diagram

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[FinanceTracker] --> B[FinanceHeader]
    A --> C[BalanceCard]
    A --> D[SummaryCards]
    A --> E[SpendingChart]
    A --> F[TransactionList]
    A --> G[TransactionForm]
    
    G --> H[CategorySelect]
    G --> I[Quick Templates]
    
    E --> J[Recharts PieChart]
    F --> K[TransactionItem]
    
    A --> L[SettingsModal]
    L --> M[TemplateManager]
    L --> N[Backup System]
    
    O[LocalStorage] --> A
    O --> L
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Real-time Transaction Management**:
- Instant transaction logging with intelligent categorization
- Quick template system for common expense categories
- Recurring transaction support with automatic generation
- Real-time balance calculations and budget forecasting

**Advanced Visualization**:
- Interactive pie charts showing spending distribution
- Category-wise spending breakdown with percentage indicators
- Forecasting system based on current spending patterns
- Responsive design optimized for mobile device usage

**Data Management**:
- Local-first architecture with automatic backup capabilities
- Import/export functionality for data portability
- Multi-currency support with real-time conversion rates
- Persistent settings and preferences across sessions

### Implementation Highlights

**Smart Transaction Entry** (`components/transaction-form.tsx`):
- Clipboard parsing for smart amount extraction
- Mathematical expression evaluation for complex calculations
- Quick template system with customizable categories
- Mobile-optimized input handling with focus management

**Financial Intelligence** (`components/finance-tracker.tsx`):
- Automated recurring transaction generation
- Monthly budget planning with adjustable limits
- Historical data browsing and comparison
- Currency conversion with base UAH storage

**Data Persistence Strategy** (`lib/data-transfer.ts`):
- Structured backup format with version control
- Atomic import operations with rollback capability
- Efficient data serialization for large datasets
- Cross-browser compatibility for localStorage operations

### Feature Architecture Diagram

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Idle
    Idle --> AddingTransaction: User Input
    AddingTransaction --> Validating: Form Submission
    Validating --> Saving: Validation Passes
    Validating --> Error: Validation Fails
    Saving --> Idle: Transaction Saved
    Error --> AddingTransaction: Fix Input
    
    Idle --> ViewingData: Navigation
    ViewingData --> Filtering: Apply Filters
    Filtering --> ViewingData: Results Displayed
    
    Idle --> ManagingSettings: Settings Access
    ManagingSettings --> ExportingData: Export Action
    ManagingSettings --> ImportingData: Import Action
    ExportingData --> Idle: Backup Complete
    ImportingData --> Idle: Data Loaded
    
    Idle --> EditingTransaction: Edit Mode
    EditingTransaction --> Updating: Update Saved
    Updating --> Idle: Transaction Updated
```

The FinTracker application represents a sophisticated yet accessible solution for personal finance management, combining modern web technologies with practical financial tools to create an intuitive and powerful user experience.

- [finance.ts](lib/finance.ts)
- [transaction-form.tsx](components/transaction-form.tsx)
- [spending-chart.tsx](components/spending-chart.tsx)
- [layout.tsx](app/layout.tsx)
- [data-transfer.ts](lib/data-transfer.ts)

</blog>
